description: Iraq invades Kuwait; fears of supply disruption from the Persian Gulf spike prices sharply.
Currency collapses across Southeast Asia sharply reduce regional oil demand.
Prices hit a historic low amid oversupply and weak Asian demand.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers agree to cut output, beginning a multi-year price recovery.
Terrorist attacks in the US introduce geopolitical risk premium into energy markets.
US-led invasion of Iraq raises concerns over Middle East supply stability.
Gulf Coast refining and production capacity is severely disrupted.
Brent crude reaches an all-time high amid strong demand and speculative activity.
Lehman Brothers collapse triggers a global recession and demand collapse.
Political unrest spreads across the Middle East and North Africa, raising supply risk.
Conflict disrupts Libyan oil production and exports.
OPEC declines to cut output despite falling prices, accelerating a price collapse.
Implementation Day of the nuclear deal allows Iranian oil back onto world markets.
First OPEC production cut agreement since 2008, aimed at rebalancing the market.
OPEC+ talks collapse and Saudi Arabia launches a price war just as COVID-19 collapses demand.
US storage capacity constraints briefly send WTI futures prices below zero.
Invasion triggers sanctions on Russian energy exports and a global supply shock.
